properties.remark: THE EM REPORT AN APARTMENT COMPLEX IS BEING EVACUATED NEAR FORT OLGLETHORPE. SEVERAL OTHER ROADS ACROSS THE COUNTY WERE UNDER WATER.
TWO TREES DOWN ON HWY 39 ABOUT 1 MILE SOUTHWEST OF OMAHA.
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ONTO MILLER RD. CREWS ARE WORKING TO CLEAR THE ROAD.
A LARGE CEDAR TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN AT HIGHWAY 74 AND LAWRENCE RD.
A LARGE TREE WAS BLOWN ONTO PLEASANT VALLEY RD AT LAWRENCE ROAD.
A TREE AND POWERLINES WERE BLOWN DOWN ONTO LANIER RD.

ESTIMATED PEAK WIND AROUND 80 MPH. THE DAMAGE OCCURED ALONG ROCK HILL SCHOOL ROAD WHERE 30 TO 50 MEDIUM TO LARGE PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED. THE TREES WERE ALL LAYING IN ONE DIRECTION AND STRETCHED NEARLY 500 YARDS WEST TO EAST ALONG THE ROAD.
METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF A POLE BARN ON HIGHWAY 33 BETWEEN COFER AND LANDFILL ROADS.
A TREE AND POWER LINES WERE BLOWN DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF CHRISTIAN CIRCLE AND CHRISTIAN DRIVE.
A TREE WAS DOWNED ON MILLS DRIVE.
A TREE AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED AT BRADEN RD AND SALEM RD.
A TREE WAS DOWNED ON MILLS DRIVE.
REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ABOUT 1/2 MILE NORTH OF HWY 128 AND CROWELL CHURCH RD. REPORT FROM COUNTY EMA. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
CAMPER REPORTED OVERTURNED WITH NOBODY INSIDE ALONG THOMAS ROAD. ADDITIONAL REPORT OF TREE ON A ROOF ALONG THOMAS ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

AN EF-0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GA-HWY 128 AND THOMAS ROAD IN EAST-CENTRAL TAYLOR COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS ONLY ON THE GROUND FOR SEVERAL MINUTES BUT SNAPPED AND UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES AND EVEN DESTROYED A SHED. THE TORNADO SNAPPED A FEW TREES AS IT CROSSED INTO CRAWFORD COUNTY. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT AROUND 80 MPH. AN AERIAL SURVEY CONFIRMED THE END POINT OVER MAINLY SWAMP LANDS OF WESTERN CRAWFORD COUNTY.
CAMPER REPORTED OVERTURNED WITH NOBODY INSIDE ALONG THOMAS ROAD. ADDITIONAL REPORT OF TREE ON A ROOF ALONG THOMAS ROAD.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR ZENITH MILL DR AND LEE POPE RD. ONE POWER LINE ON HOME. POSSIBLE TORNADO
TWO PINE TREES DOWN ALONG KAY RD SOUTH OF BYRON. ESTIMATED WINDS 60 MPH. DAMAGE LOCATION LINES UP WITH WHERE POSSIBLE TORNADO COULD HAVE TOUCHED DOWN.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHEAST OF CENTERVILLE AND INITIALLY TOOK OUT TREES ALONG SENTRY OAKS DRIVE. AS IT MOVED EAST THE TORNADO BRIEFLY REACHED EF-1 INTENSITY WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 90 MPH AT NAPIER AVE AND FISHER ST WHERE LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...SOME FALLING ON RESIDENCES. SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST. JUST BEFORE LIFTING THE TORNADO CROSSED ROBINS AFB NEAR THE GREEN STREET GATE. A METAL MAINTENANCE HANGAR SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHEAST OF CENTERVILLE AND INITIALLY TOOK OUT TREES ALONG SENTRY OAKS DRIVE. AS IT MOVED EAST THE TORNADO BRIEFLY REACHED EF-1 INTENSITY WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS AROUND 90 MPH AT NAPIER AVE AND FISHER ST WHERE LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED...SOME FALLING ON RESIDENCES. THE WEAK TORNADO CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST CAUSING SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE. IT IS YET TO BE DETERMINED IF THE TORNADO CROSSED OVER ROBINS AIR FORCE BASE.
WARNER ROBINS ASOS STATION REPORTED PEAK GUST OF 72 KTS OUT OF SW JUST NORTH OF STRONG ROTATION SIGNATURE.
WEAK EF0 TORNADO. SAME STORM THAT CAUSED THE WARNER ROBBINS TORNADO SPUN UP BRIEFLY SNAPPING A FEW TREES ALONG BULLARD ROAD. MAX WIND 65 MPH. WIDTH 60 YARDS. LENGTH 0.6MI.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF THE CENTER OF ALLENTOWN AT 850 AM EDT. THE SHORT-LIVED TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR AROUND 1.90 MILES AND HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON MOORE ROAD WHERE ONE HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS SEEN ALONG HIGHWAY 112 JUST BEFORE THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 80 AND 112. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR U

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 95 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF THE CENTER OF ALLENTOWN AT 850 AM EDT. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR AROUND 2.40 MILES AND HAD A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 300 YARDS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS FOUND ON MOORE ROAD WHERE ONE HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE WAS ALONG HIGHWAY 112 BEFORE THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 80 AND 112. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

THE HALL COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTED 53 HOMES DAMAGED WEST OF OAKWOOD. NINE HOMES RECEIVED MAJOR DAMAGE AND 5 WERE MOSTLY DESTROYED. ALL OF THE DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY TREES FALLING ON THE HOMES. DAMAGED HOMES WERE NOTED ON RAINTREE CIRCLE...RAINTREE TRACE...FLAT CREEK ROAD...STEPHENS ROAD...HIDDEN HARBOR...BYERS ROAD...STONE TRACE...POINT SOUTH DRIVE...KINGS COURT...FLATSTONE DRIVE AND COBBLESTONE.

SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED SOME LEVEL OF DAMAGE. THE WORST OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A HOME WHERE BOTH GARAGE DOORS COLLAPSED INWARD AND BLEW A PORTION OF THE ROOF OFF. DEBRIS FROM THIS ROOF WAS THROWN MORE THAN 500 YARDS DOWNSTREAM. THIS SUPPORTS MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MPH A HIGH END EF-0 TORNADO.
